operating_state: Total revenues increased 15% to $402.8 billion driven by Google Services growth and Google Cloud acceleration. Google Search revenues rose from increased queries and advertiser spending, while YouTube ads grew across direct response and brand products. Google Cloud maintained robust enterprise adoption momentum with infrastructure and platform services expansion. Revenue backlog reached $242.8 billion with over 50% expected within 24 months, providing strong forward visibility.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased 11% from higher content acquisition costs, depreciation, and traffic acquisition costs, with TAC rate improving from revenue mix shift toward Search properties. Operating expenses surged 22% driven by stock-based compensation increases, European Commission fine, and legal settlement accrual. Substantial AI infrastructure investment exceeding $200 billion over five years supporting custom TPU development and specialized compute capacity expansion.
material_events: European Commission imposed fine for advertising technology practices, bringing total outstanding EC penalties to approximately $8.6 billion under appeal. Entered definitive agreements to acquire Wiz and Intersect, both expected to close in 2026. Executed $45.4 billion share repurchases, increased quarterly dividend 5% to $0.21 per share, and issued senior unsecured notes.
macro_exposures: Supply chain constraints affecting specialized AI chip manufacturing and server equipment from limited qualified suppliers, with energy supply limitations constraining data center expansion globally. OECD global minimum tax implementation across multiple countries could increase effective tax rate. Geopolitical tensions including ongoing Middle East and Ukraine conflicts creating potential operational disruptions and increased cybersecurity risks.
forward_outlook: Management expects significant increases in technical infrastructure investment for 2026 relative to 2025, particularly servers, network equipment, and data centers supporting AI offerings. Capital expenditures increased to $91.4 billion in 2025 with continued heavy investment planned in AI infrastructure and custom TPU advancement. Strategic focus on scaling Other Bets commercialization while maintaining R&D investment across transportation and life sciences verticals.
uncertainty_profile: Intensifying regulatory scrutiny across AI development, competition policy, and data privacy with pending antitrust remedies potentially requiring structural changes to advertising technology operations. Over 1,000 AI-related bills considered by US state legislatures in 2025, with EU AI Act fully effective creating transparency and safety requirements for general purpose AI systems. Unrecognized tax benefits with ongoing examinations across multiple jurisdictions, while intellectual property litigation exposure continues.
